boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Sandbox environment creation, code execution, and deletion as described in the Sandbox Quickstart

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer runs code in a Koyeb sandbox and observes expected output, confirming environment setup and execution capability.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Terminal output shows 'Hello from Python!' printed after running the sandboxed Python script.
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully deploy and run a sandbox environment that executes a simple Python script and outputs 'Hello from Python!'
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This milestone is explicitly documented as a quickstart success scenario demonstrating the ability to create, run, and delete a sandbox environment with code execution, representing a clear first success.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has internet access and a modern web browser
Developer has basic knowledge of deploying applications or running code
Developer has an email address for account registration
entry_point.developer_intent: Create a free Koyeb account and deploy a simple application or sandbox environment to verify the platform's functionality.

friction_gates.description: User must create a Koyeb account to access the platform and sandbox features.
User must generate an API token in the control panel to authenticate SDK usage.
Python must be installed locally to run the sandbox example code.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Koyeb account with Starter, Pro, or Scale Plan to access sandbox features.
Install Python on the local machine to run the sandbox example code.
Generate a Koyeb API token from the control panel to authenticate SDK usage.

primary_path.action: Sign up for a free Koyeb account
details: Navigate to Koyeb documentation or homepage
Click sign up and complete registration
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Registration errors or email verification required
primary_path.input: Email and password or OAuth credentials

primary_path.action: Set up local environment for sandbox example
details: Create and activate Python virtual environment
primary_path.output: Isolated Python environment with Koyeb SDK installed
primary_path.success_signal: No errors during environment setup and SDK installation

primary_path.action: Generate Koyeb API token in control panel
details: Create API token with name and description

primary_path.action: Set environment variable KOYEB_API_TOKEN with the generated token
Create Python script main.py with sandbox creation and execution code
details: Write code to create sandbox, write file, execute script, print output, and delete sandbox

primary_path.failure_or_wait: Errors in sandbox creation or execution
primary_path.output: Terminal logs showing sandbox creation, execution, and deletion

evidence_supported: Step-by-step sandbox creation and code execution
sections_used: Managing Sandboxes with the Koyeb Python and JavaScript SDKs
sources.title: Sandbox Quickstart | Koyeb

alternatives_considered.name: Koyeb CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: While the CLI is mentioned as a management tool, the primary documented onboarding and first success steps focus on the web console and control panel interfaces.

alternatives_considered.name: Koyeb API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The API is available for programmatic interaction, but initial onboarding and first success documented steps emphasize the web console and SDK usage.

surface.name: Koyeb Web Console
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation and quickstart guides primarily describe onboarding via the Koyeb web console and control panel for deploying applications and managing sandboxes.
